hero.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
hero.prob_take:
At 25.5%, traders are pricing in roughly a 1-in-4 shot that the US and Iran sign a permanent peace deal in the next 16 months, which feels generous given the two countries haven't even held serious talks since Trump won the election.

The 10-point surge in 24 hours suggests retail panic buying on headlines rather than any material shift in diplomatic reality, making this a fade for anyone who remembers that "imminent breakthroughs" in Iran relations have been the market's favorite pump fake for a decade.

daily_take.headline: Iran Peace Deal Jumps 10 Points. Smart Money Sees Diplomatic Opening.
daily_take.deck: US-Iran permanent peace odds spiked from 15.5% to 25.5% in 24 hours on $7.3M volume, signaling real conviction from informed traders. The market is pricing in either fresh diplomatic signals or a shift in geopolitical calculus. At 25.5%, this is still cheap if backchannels are actually moving, but traders should wait for confirmation before chasing. Watch uranium surrender odds (41.5%, up 13.3pts) as a proxy for de-escalation momentum.

sidebar.headline: Uranium surrender deal is pricing the confidence first. If enriched stockpile talks are real, peace odds should follow higher still.
sidebar.label: Iran Uranium Surrender: 41.5%

sidebar.headline: UFC fights don't move 13.5pts on soft logic. 50% for Nascimento-Raposo looks fairly balanced; sharp bettors are taking both sides.
sidebar.label: Nascimento vs. Raposo: 50.0%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic recovery at 27.5% is the real tell. If shipping normalizes by April, the peace deal becomes much more likely. Watch this number.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Traffic Normal: 27.5%
